zh.lifeSummary: 當Robert de Stafford於大約1247年出生在英國德比夏爾艾吉頓時，他的父親Robert de Stafford32歲，母親Alice de Corbet23歲。他和Gundreda de Stafford至少有1個兒子和5個女兒。他去世於大約1303年在英國英格蘭斯塔福爾安斯洛福享年58歲，埋葬在英國德比夏爾艾吉頓。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Stafford
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name principally from Stafford (Staffordshire), but occasionally from other places with similar names, such as Stafford House in Ifield (Sussex), possibly East and West Stowford in East Down (Devon), and three minor places in Devon called Stafford, in Dolton, Broadhembury, and Colyton parishes. The places are all named with Old English ford ‘ford’ as the final element, but have different initial elements. The Staffordshire placename has Old English stæth ‘river bank, shore’; Stafford in Colyton (Devon) has Old English stān ‘stone, rock’; the Sussex placename has Old English stēor ‘steer, bullock’; East and West Stowford (Devon) have Old English stæf ‘staff, stave, rod’; Stafford in Dolton and Stafford in Broadhembury (Devon) may have Old English stæth, stān, or stæf.

variants.name: Stocker
meanings: German and Swiss German (also Stöcker): topographic name for someone living by a tree stump (see Stock 3) or an occupational name for a tree cutter, from Middle High German stocken ‘to clear land’.
German and Swiss German (also Stöcker); Dutch: occupational name from Middle High German, Middle Dutch stocker ‘jailer’.

English: occupational name from Middle English stokker ‘one who sells stockfish’ (fish dried in the air without salt). This was the usual source of the name in medieval London, where a bylaw of 1419 stated that no stokker should board a ship to buy fish (presumably in order to forestall the market).

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: When Robert de Stafford was born about 1247, in Egginton, Derbyshire, England, his father, Robert de Stafford, was 32 and his mother, Alice de Corbet, was 23. He had at least 1 son and 5 daughters with Gundreda de Stafford. He died about 1303, in Anslow, Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 58, and was buried in Egginton, Derbyshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Robert de Stafford est né environ 1247, à Egginton, Derbyshire, Angleterre, son père, Robert de Stafford, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Alice de Corbet, avait 23 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils et 5 filles avec Gundreda de Stafford. Il est décédé environ 1303, à Anslow, Staffordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 58 ans, et a été enterré à Egginton, Derbyshire, Angleterre.

de.Burton upon Trent, Derbyshire, England: Burton upon Trent St Modwen, Derbyshire, England, United Kingdom
de.lifeSummary: Als Robert de Stafford um 1247 in Egginton, Derbyshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Robert de Stafford 32 und seine Mutter Alice de Corbet 23. Er hatte mit Gundreda de Stafford mindestens 1 Sohn und 5 Töchter. Er starb um 1303 im Alter von 58 Jahren in Anslow, Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Egginton, Derbyshire, England begraben.

Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio e 5 figlie con Gundreda de Stafford. Morì circa 1303, nel Anslow, Staffordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 58 anni, e fu sepolto a Egginton, Derbyshire, England.

ko.lifeSummary: Robert de Stafford(이)가 대략 1247년 영국 더비실르 긴턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Robert de Stafford(은)는 32세였고, 그의 어머니 Alice de Corbet(은)는 23세였다. 그는 Gundreda de Stafford(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 대략 1303년 영국 잉글랜드 스태퍼드실르 앤슬로에서 58세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 더비실르 긴턴에 묻혔다.

Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho e 5 filhas com Gundreda de Stafford. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Robert de Stafford родился около 1247 г. в местности эгинтон, дирбишир, Англия, его отцу по имени Robert de Stafford было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Alice de Corbet, 23. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын и 5 дочерей от Gundreda de Stafford. Он умер около 1303 г. в местности анслоу, стаффордшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 58 лет и был похоронен в местности эгинтон, дирбишир, Англия.
